Empires & Puzzles Franz Hero Review: The 4-Star Titan King & Rush Attack Monster

Don't let the 4-star rarity fool you. Franz is a meta-defining powerhouse for Titans and Rush Attack modes. With health stats that rival legendaries and a unique damage amplification mechanic, this Clash of Knights hero punches way above his weight class. We break down why he's essential for high scores.
Franz: The 4-Star Hero Disguised as a Titan God
Franz: The 4-Star Behemoth Who Teaches Titans Fear

Meet Franz, the 4-star Nature hero from the Clash of Knights Alliance Quest who looked at the 5-star roster, laughed, and decided to do their job better. Released in early 2022, Franz is a Paladin who operates at Very Slow speed. While "Very Slow" usually sends players running for the hills, Franz is the exception to the rule. He is arguably one of the most important 4-star heroes in the entire game, largely due to a status ailment that significantly amplifies all incoming damage. He doesn't just hit enemies; he opens the floodgates for your entire team to annihilate them. He is a staple for high-level Titan hits and a nightmare in Rush Attack formats.

Franz is a visual fortress. Encased in ornate, teal and gold armor that leaves almost nothing exposed, he looks less like a man and more like a walking castle tower. The design screams "durability," fitting perfectly with his massive health pool and Paladin class. His weapon, a glowing energy lance, hints at the magical nature of his "Sonorous Spear" ability. It is a commanding design that fits the Raven family aesthetic—stoic, heavily armored, and slightly intimidating. He clearly didn't skip leg day, or armor day for that matter.

Franz possesses a stat distribution that was revolutionary for a 4-star upon his release and remains impressive today. His 1519 Health is astronomical for an Epic hero, rivaling and even exceeding many older 5-star heroes. His Defense is also solid at 682. The trade-off is his Attack stat, which sits at a lowly 552.

Even in the current meta of 2026, Franz's bulk holds up remarkably well. While modern power creep has introduced 4-star heroes with higher overall power and stronger attack stats (often pushing 700+ or even 800+ with costumes and limit breaks), Franz's raw survivability is still top-tier for his rarity. He was built to last, and he does exactly that. His low attack is negligible because his primary role is not dealing direct damage, but amplifying the damage of others.
At Very Slow speed and with low attack, this is the least interesting part of the skill. It's chip damage, essentially.
This is the crown jewel. This ailment makes all enemies take 50% more damage from

sources—tiles, special skills, minions, and items. Unlike Defense Down, this stacks with almost everything (except similar specific damage-increasing ailments like Motega's). This specific mechanic is what allows for record-breaking Titan scores and team wipes in Rush wars.
Preventing enemies from receiving new buffs for 6 turns is an eternity in this game. It completely shuts down supports, taunters, and healers who rely on buffs (like HoT).
Let's break down the mechanics that make Franz a mathematical monster.
Very Fast (6.5 tiles). This is where he shifts from a utility piece to a terrifying weapon of mass destruction.
Instant. This circumvents his speed drawback entirely.
Imagine a sniper hits for 1000 damage.
With Defense Down (-34%): Damage might increase to ~1500 (rough estimate).
With Elemental Defense Down (-54%): Damage increases further.
You take that final calculated number and multiply it by 1.5.
of the damage calculation chain is why he is so potent. If you have a cascade of tiles that would normally do 10,000 damage, Franz turns that into 15,000 damage instantly.
6 turns. Most ailments last 3 or 4 turns. 6 turns gives you a massive window to dump tiles into a Titan or recharge your other heroes in a war.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Raven Family brings a unique mechanic to the table that can turn a battle in your favor unexpectedly.
The Raven family bonus offers a chance (35%/50% for 2/3 heroes) to make the ailment cast by the hero
. If Franz fires and this bonus procs, the enemy team is stuck taking +50% damage and receiving no buffs for 6 turns with absolutely no way to cleanse it. This is devastating.
Franz does not have specific passive skills outside of the family bonus effects often associated with the event heroes.

The "Vampire" Aether Power heals the hero for a percentage of normal damage dealt. Given Franz's low attack stat and very slow speed, this is arguably one of the weaker powers for him. He isn't dealing consistent normal damage to sustain himself meaningfully. A defensive power would have been preferred, but any extra sustain is better than nothing.

When Franz was released, he immediately broke the ceiling for Titan scores. Players realized that he was essentially a "5-star in disguise" due to his stats and unique debuff. He became a must-have for every Titan team, not just against Blue Titans.

While stats have crept up, no other hero has completely replaced Franz's specific niche of "Universal +50% Damage Increase + Buff Block for 6 Turns" at the 4-star level. There are other damage boosters (like Buster or certain 5-stars), but Franz remains the most accessible and reliable option for many.

Franz is still a top-tier hero for PvE (Titans, Challenge Events, Tower Events) and specialized PvP modes (Rush Attack). He has not been relegated to the bench; he sits comfortably in the "Hall of Fame" of heroes who defy their rarity. Veteran players still limit break him twice because his utility is timeless.

At the time of writing, Franz does not have a costume. His base form is powerful enough that he hasn't desperately needed one to stay relevant, though a costume providing a stat boost would certainly be welcomed by the community to keep his survivability in line with the latest legendary monsters.
Franz is a support hero who needs to survive long enough to fire his very slow skill. Once he fires, his job is largely done. Therefore, survivability is paramount.
Maximizes his already high defense and health. Ensures he survives slashes from 14* Titans and heavy hits in Rush wars.
Low tile damage contribution (though his skill buffs tile damage anyway).
Slightly increases tile damage against Blue Titans.
Makes him significantly squishier. Given his Very Slow speed, if he dies before firing, he is useless.
(Defense/Health) is strictly recommended. Franz's value is in his special skill, not his tile damage. Keeping him alive to cast that skill is the only priority.
The +50% damage received debuff is one of the strongest offensive tools in the game for high scores.
Shutting down enemy buffs for 6 turns is a massive control ability.
Base stats for health and defense are exceptional for a 4-star.
Can be used on Titan teams of any color (except against Green reflect) because his debuff is valuable enough to sacrifice a color-matched tile slot.
Outside of Rush Attack and Mana Potion usage, he is painfully slow to charge.
He hits like a wet noodle directly; his damage comes from his team capitalizing on his debuff.
He often requires items (mana pots) to be effective in standard PvE play.
His absolute best role. He is a staple for players pushing for 100k+ hits.
He becomes a Very Fast hero who shuts down the enemy team and makes them paper-thin.
Essential for high scores on boss waves. Mana pot him up, fire, and watch the bosses melt.
The damage boost is crucial for reaching the cumulative damage rewards.
Franz is a force multiplier. He shouldn't be your only threat; he should be the one setting up your threats.
Tarlak/Miki (Attack Boost) + Evelyn/Almur (EDD) + Frigg/Buddy (DD) + Franz (Dmg Amp) + High Attack Green Sniper.
This stacks every damage modifier available: Attack Up, Elemental Defense Down, General Defense Down, and Franz's Damage Increase.
Position Franz in the center or flank in Rush wars. If he fires, the attacker cannot buff themselves (no defense up, no HoT) and takes massive damage from your snipers or AoE hitters.
For difficult event stages, firing Franz first ensures your hitters deal 1.5x damage, often clearing the boss wave in a single rotation of specials.
If Franz hits a Taunt hero, the debuff only applies to them (unless he is paired with a dispeller). However, his buff block prevents them from refreshing taunt.
If the enemy blocks ailments, Franz does nothing but tickle them.
A fast cleanser can undo his work immediately (unless the Raven family bonus makes it uncleansable).
If they dodge the skill, they dodge the debuff.

If you pull Franz, lock him immediately. Even if you don't have the materials to max him yet, he is a long-term investment. Prioritize leveling him for your Titan teams. Even at 3/60, he can be useful on lower-level Titans if protected with items. Do not feed him away.

You likely already have him Limit Broken. Is he worth the Alpha Aethers for LB2? While his Aether power isn't great, the stat boost helps keep him alive against modern 5-star defenses in Rush wars and against 14* Titans. If you use him daily on Titans and in every Rush war, the investment is justified solely for the stats.
The community recognized Franz's value instantly, dubbing him a "5-star in disguise."
"Franz is your new favourite Titan Hero... Basically with his ailment on you can take your Titan scores and increase them by 50%."
In Very Fast tournaments, the sentiment is clear:
"This guy is a win button... 6 green tiles for Franz before they can fire and they do nothing."
Many players compared his impact to legendary heroes, with one user noting,
"He is more valuable than most 5-star. I’d make him the priority."
Some players felt his impact was so strong it felt unfair:
"Unless a completely terrible board happens, it feels like cheating."

Franz is the definition of "utility over rarity." He proves that you don't need to be a shiny new 5-star to dominate the leaderboard. Whether you are fighting a Mythic Titan or defending in a Rush War, Franz brings a unique toolkit that amplifies your entire roster's potential. He is the ultimate force multiplier, turning good hits into great ones and great hits into record-breakers. If you have him, level him. If you don't, save your gems for the next time the Knights clash.
A mandatory hero for anyone serious about Titans and events. A cornerstone of the 4-star roster.

A deep dive into Franz, the 4-Star Nature hero from the Raven Family. Discover why his very slow speed is worth the wait, how he revolutionizes Titan scores with his damage amplification, and the best strategies for using him in Rush Attack tournaments and wars.
